title: activity — rollout flags
description: Rollout / change-management flags for THIS module. Distinct from `features.json` (business-tier features gated by entitlement + plan). Every material change ships behind a flag per SOC 2 CC8.1.

flags.description: Master kill-switch. Off = LogsActivity trait no-ops silently on every domain model. Existing rows still readable. Emergency freeze for a runaway write path (e.g. a domain model's overzealous logOnly() suddenly firing thousands of activities per request).
Toggles WebSocket broadcast to tenant.{id}.activity + user.{id}.activity. Off = frontend polls. Incident kill-switch for the reverb layer.

Toggles the request-scoped ActivityBatcher. Off = every activity surfaces individually with no batch_uuid. Used when the batcher's cache reads are causing memory pressure OR the collapsed rendering is confusing users.

Rollout guard for the retention policy write path. Off by default; enterprise entitlement flips on. Provides an extra kill-switch beyond the entitlement so we can freeze retention writes tenant-wide during an incident.
Rollout guard for daily / weekly digest emails. Independent of the entitlement so ops can freeze digests if the scheduler is misbehaving.

Rollout guard for ArchiveActivitiesToColdStorageJob. Off in v1 — extended-retention rows stay on the hot Postgres table until cold-archive rolls out. Canary bucket enables cold archive for one tenant per canary group first.

One-shot flag for the Wave-6 upgrade. When on for a tenant, BackfillActivityTenantIdFromJsonbJob runs on the tenant. Flipped on per-tenant during phased rollout, then decommissioned once every tenant has completed the backfill.
Rollout guard for the monthly feature-flag review report (matches grants' equivalent). Off in Wave 6 — surfaces exist but the scheduler + notification wiring lands in a later wave.

retirement_policy.description: A flag lives at most 90 days after reaching 100% GA. After 90d, either the flag is deleted OR converts to a `kill-switch`. Monthly review command prints ages.
